changes: Updated Brent price range to $105-$112 (volatile; $111.69 on Apr 2). Added ~2,000 vessels trapped in Gulf (IMO data). Updated UN Hormuz vote to Saturday Apr 5 (Bahrain resolution: 'all defensive means' for 6 months; China/Russia vetoes expected). Added Trump Apr 3 statement ('hasn't even started destroying what's left'). Added LG Chem Yeosu No. 2 cracker shutdown (sd-029, 800k tpa, Mar 23). Updated OPEC+ Apr 5 context ('most consequential meeting since alliance formation'). Added S&P Global 'Persian Gulf LNG supply scenarios: Longer market impacts from war' (Apr 2) to Qatar sources. Updated energy-news-data.json with latest headlines. Fixed day count references (Day 36). No new FM declarations found in last 48 hours.
newEvents: Trump on Apr 3: 'US hasn't even started destroying what's left in Iran' — vows more infrastructure strikes (Al-Monitor)
~2,000 vessels trapped in Persian Gulf per IMO (CNN liveblog Apr 2)
UN Hormuz vote confirmed for Saturday Apr 5 — Bahrain resolution authorizes 'all defensive means necessary' for at least 6 months (Al-Monitor, France 24, Washington Post)
China opposes force authorization — vetoes expected; Russia also opposes (Al-Monitor, CNN)
Brent volatile $105-$112; $111.69 on Apr 2 (Fortune); Apr 3 surging on bridge strikes
Kuwait air defenses intercepting missiles twice on Apr 3 (Wikipedia/ACLED)
S&P Global published 'Persian Gulf LNG supply scenarios update: Longer market impacts from war' (Apr 2)
OPEC+ Apr 5 described as 'most consequential meeting since alliance formation' (Middle East Insider)
LG Chem shut 800k tpa Yeosu No. 2 ethylene cracker due to naphtha shortage (Seoul Economic Daily, Mar 23)
South Korea turns to Russian naphtha for chip supply chain (The Next Web)
newShutdowns: sd-029: LG Chem Yeosu No. 2 Cracker (South Korea, Mar 23, 800k tpa ethylene — naphtha supply shortage)
noNewFmReason: No new force majeure declarations found since Formosa Plastics Corp (fm-027, Apr 1). LG Chem and Hanwha Solutions notified customers of potential FM on some products (Korea Times). Industry warns April will see cascade of FM declarations as naphtha inventories deplete.
